description: Expand the intervention system to unified 'Operations' section. Phase 1 adds Reminders (rappels) with RRULE recurrence support. Includes DB migration, repository/service layer, CRUD actions, UI (segment control, list, cards, wizard, detail page), Vercel Cron for recurrence scan, and dashboard widget. Navbar renamed from 'Interventions' to 'Operations'.

userStories.title: DB Migration: reminders + recurrence_rules + recurrence_occurrences
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the database schema for reminders and the RRULE recurrence system with RLS, indexes, triggers, and the batch scan RPC
acceptanceCriteria: reminders table created with all columns from design (title, description, priority, status, entity links, due_date, assignation, recurrence FKs)
CHECK constraint on priority (basse/normale/haute), status (en_attente/en_cours/termine/annule)
XOR constraint: num_nonnulls(building_id, lot_id, contact_id, contract_id) <= 1
recurrence_rules table with rrule TEXT, dtstart, source_type, source_template JSONB, auto_create, notify_days_before
recurrence_occurrences table with occurrence_date, status, generated_entity_type/id, team_id
All 3 tables have team_id NOT NULL + RLS policies (is_team_manager pattern)
updated_at triggers on reminders and recurrence_rules
Indexes on team_id, due_date, status, assigned_to for reminders; active rules and pending occurrences for recurrence tables
RPC scan_pending_recurrences(look_ahead_days) SECURITY DEFINER with batch JOIN query
npm run supabase:types regenerates types
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: Migration 20260319200000. 3 tables + 8 indexes + 2 triggers + 5x3 RLS policies + 1 SECURITY DEFINER RPC. Types regenerated.

userStories.title: Repository + Service: reminder.repository.ts + reminder.service.ts
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the data access and business logic layers for reminders following the existing Repository Pattern
acceptanceCriteria: reminder.repository.ts with findByTeam(teamId, filters), findById(id), create(data), update(id, data), softDelete(id), findByAssignedTo(userId), getCountsByStatus(teamId)
reminder.service.ts with getByTeam(), getById(), create(), update(), complete(), cancel(), getStats()
recurrence.repository.ts with findActiveByTeam(teamId), findBySourceEntity(type, id), create(data), deactivate(id)
recurrence-occurrence.repository.ts with findPendingByRule(ruleId), updateStatus(id, status), linkGeneratedEntity(id, type, entityId)
All repositories use typed Supabase client (no direct DB calls in service)
Service uses after() for notification deferral
Types exported from lib/services/index.ts
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: Types in lib/types/reminder.types.ts. ReminderRepository + RecurrenceRuleRepository + RecurrenceOccurrenceRepository with factory functions. ReminderService with full CRUD + stats. All exported from lib/services/index.ts.

userStories.title: Server actions: CRUD rappels + notifications
userStories.description: As a gestionnaire, I can create, update, complete, and cancel reminders via server actions
acceptanceCriteria: createReminderAction(formData) creates reminder with Zod validation
updateReminderAction(id, formData) updates reminder fields
completeReminderAction(id) sets status=termine + completed_at
cancelReminderAction(id) sets status=annule
All actions use getServerActionAuthContextOrNull('gestionnaire')
All actions broadcast invalidation(['reminders']) via data invalidation
Activity log entries created for create/complete/cancel
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: reminder-actions.ts with 7 actions (create/update/start/complete/cancel/delete/getStats). Zod validation, XOR constraint check, after() deferred activity logs. Uses entity_type='intervention' with metadata.reminder_id until enum is extended.

userStories.title: Data invalidation: add reminders entity type
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the data invalidation broadcast system to support reminders
acceptanceCriteria: DataEntity type in lib/data-invalidation.ts includes 'reminders'
useReminders hook created in hooks/ that subscribes to onInvalidation(['reminders'])
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: Added 'reminders' to DataEntity in lib/data-invalidation.ts. Created hooks/use-reminders.ts with refreshKey pattern matching existing hooks.

userStories.title: Rename navbar Interventions → Operations + FAB + redirects
userStories.description: As a gestionnaire, the navbar shows 'Operations' instead of 'Interventions' with a dropdown for creating interventions or reminders
acceptanceCriteria: gestionnaire-sidebar.tsx: label changed to 'Operations', href to '/gestionnaire/taches'
createOptions dropdown with 'Nouvelle intervention' and 'Nouveau rappel'
gestionnaire-fab-wrapper.tsx: FAB updated with reminder creation option
Redirect middleware or next.config.js: /gestionnaire/interventions/* → /gestionnaire/taches/* (301)
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: Sidebar renamed to Operations with createOptions dropdown. FAB updated with onCreateReminder. Redirects added in next.config.js (301 permanent).

userStories.title: TaskTypeSegment component + Operations list page
userStories.description: As a gestionnaire, I see a pill-based segment control to switch between Interventions and Rappels on the Operations page
acceptanceCriteria: TaskTypeSegment component in components/operations/ with pill switcher (Interventions/Rappels)
app/gestionnaire/(with-navbar)/taches/page.tsx Server Component with getServerAuthContext
tasks-client.tsx with segment state management, renders InterventionsNavigator when segment=intervention
Segment pills show count badges per type
Mobile: pills are scrollable horizontally with icons only (labels hidden)
ContentNavigator tabs adapt to selected type (intervention statuses vs reminder statuses)
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: TaskTypeSegment with pill switcher (blue/amber), accessible role=tablist. Operations page at /gestionnaire/taches/ with parallel data fetch. URL-synced segment state via replaceState. InterventionsPageClient rendered as sibling (no double-wrap).

userStories.title: ReminderCard + RemindersNavigator + list view
userStories.description: As a gestionnaire, I can see my reminders in a card list with amber left-border accent, priority badges, and due date indicators
acceptanceCriteria: ReminderCard component with amber border-l-4, priority badge, due date with overdue indicator
Recurrence icon (RefreshCw) shown when recurrence_rule_id is set
RemindersNavigator wraps ContentNavigator with reminder-specific tabs (Toutes/En attente/En cours/Terminées)
RemindersViewContainer with card grid layout matching intervention cards
Quick actions on card: Terminer, Annuler (via dot menu)
Empty state for no reminders
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: ReminderCard with amber border, priority badges, overdue indicators, recurrence icon, quick actions. RemindersNavigator with 4 status tabs. RemindersViewContainer grid. operations-page-client wired with server action handlers + broadcast invalidation.

userStories.title: Wizard rappel (3 steps): Bien → Rappel → Confirmation
userStories.description: As a gestionnaire, I can create a new reminder through a 3-step wizard
acceptanceCriteria: reminderSteps added to lib/step-configurations.ts
Route: app/gestionnaire/(no-navbar)/taches/nouveau-rappel/
Step 1: PropertySelector with optional toggle 'Lier à un bien'
Step 2: Title (required), description, due date (DatePicker), priority (ToggleGroup basse/normale/haute), assignation (team gestionnaires select)
Step 3: Confirmation summary with all fields displayed
Submit calls createReminderAction, navigates to detail page on success
Success toast notification
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: 3-step wizard at /gestionnaire/taches/nouveau-rappel/. Step 1: optional PropertySelector with XOR. Step 2: title/desc/date/priority/assignee with DatePicker + gestionnaires fetch. Step 3: confirmation summary + createReminderAction submit. reminderSteps added to step-configurations.ts.

userStories.title: Reminder detail page (simplified layout)
userStories.description: As a gestionnaire, I can view and manage a reminder from its detail page with a simplified single-column layout
acceptanceCriteria: Route: app/gestionnaire/(no-navbar)/taches/rappels/[id]/
Server Component with getServerAuthContext('gestionnaire')
ReminderDetailsCard: due date, priority, status, linked entity, assignee, description
ReminderNotesCard: textarea for notes + list of previous notes (reuse CommentsCard pattern)
Activity tab with activity log entries
Action buttons: Commencer, Terminer, Annuler (status-dependent)
Single column layout (no sidebar)
Back navigation to Operations page
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: Detail page at /gestionnaire/taches/rappels/[id]/ with single-column layout. Status-dependent action buttons, amber accent card, notes textarea, overdue indicator, recurrence badge. Activity section placeholder. Loading skeleton.

userStories.title: Recurrence config UI + rrule.js integration
userStories.description: As a gestionnaire, I can configure recurrence when creating a reminder (toggle + frequency picker + end condition)
acceptanceCriteria: rrule package installed (npm install rrule)
RecurrenceConfig component in components/recurrence/ with toggle, frequency selector, day picker, end condition
Frequencies: Quotidien, Hebdomadaire, Mensuel, Annuel, Personnalisé
Weekly: day multi-select (L M Me J V S D)
Monthly: radio 'Le X du mois' / 'Le Xe jour du mois'
End: Jamais / Après X occurrences / À la date
Component outputs valid RRULE string
Integrated into wizard rappel Step 2 (behind recurrence toggle)
createReminderAction extended: creates recurrence_rule + generates first batch of occurrences via rrule.js
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: RecurrenceConfig with toggle, frequency, weekly day picker, monthly mode, interval, end condition. RRULE built client-side without rrule.js. Server-side rrule.js generates first 10 occurrences. Bidirectional parsing. Live French summary preview. Integrated in wizard Step 2 + confirmation Step 3.

userStories.title: Vercel Cron: recurrence scan + notifications
userStories.description: As a system, a daily cron job scans pending recurrence occurrences and generates notifications or auto-creates reminders
acceptanceCriteria: app/api/cron/recurrence-scan/route.ts created following intervention-reminders cron pattern
Calls scan_pending_recurrences RPC with service role client
For auto_create=true: creates reminder instance from source_template, links occurrence
For auto_create=false: creates in-app notification for the team manager
Updates occurrence status to 'notified' or 'confirmed'
Vercel cron config in vercel.json: schedule '0 6 * * *'
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: Cron at /api/cron/recurrence-scan. CRON_SECRET auth, scan_pending_recurrences RPC with 7-day look-ahead. Auto-create reminders from source_template or notify team managers. Vercel.json updated with 0 6 * * * schedule.

userStories.title: Dashboard: reminder counter widget
userStories.description: As a gestionnaire, I see a reminder counter widget on my dashboard showing today's reminders and overdue count
acceptanceCriteria: New widget on gestionnaire dashboard: 'Rappels' with count today + count overdue
Widget clickable → navigates to /gestionnaire/taches with segment=rappel pre-filtered
Uses reminder.service.getStats(teamId) for counts
Styled consistently with existing dashboard widgets
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: ReminderStatsWidget in components/operations/. Server-side stats via createServerReminderService().getStats(). Promise.allSettled for fault tolerance. Conditional rendering when counts > 0. Overdue red ring highlight. Wired into manager-dashboard-v2 and async-dashboard-content.
